STRENGTH.primaryObjective: Increase maximal force production and improve performance on compound lifts.
STRENGTH.recommendedFrequency: 2-3 sessions per muscle group per week
STRENGTH.warmupRecommendation: Perform general warm-up, mobility work and progressive ramp-up sets before working sets.
STRENGTH.failureGuidelines: Avoid failure on heavy compound lifts. Isolation exercises may approach failure occasionally.
STRENGTH.deloadRecommendation: Every 6-8 weeks or when fatigue accumulates significantly.
notes: Increase weight only after maintaining good form.
Longer rest periods improve strength output.

HYPERTROPHY.primaryObjective: Maximize skeletal muscle growth through sufficient volume and progressive overload.
HYPERTROPHY.recommendedFrequency: 2 sessions per muscle group per week
HYPERTROPHY.tempo: Controlled throughout full range of motion
HYPERTROPHY.warmupRecommendation: General warm-up followed by activation and ramp-up sets.
HYPERTROPHY.failureGuidelines: Compound exercises should generally stop 1-2 reps before failure. Isolation movements may safely reach failure.
HYPERTROPHY.deloadRecommendation: Every 6-10 weeks depending on fatigue and recovery.
notes: Nutrition and recovery are essential for growth.

ENDURANCE.primaryObjective: Improve the ability of muscles to sustain repeated contractions over time.
ENDURANCE.recommendedFrequency: 2-4 sessions per muscle group per week
ENDURANCE.failureGuidelines: Avoid complete failure to preserve workout quality.
notes: Maintain exercise quality throughout the session.
Conditioning can be incorporated between sets.

WEIGHT_LOSS.primaryObjective: Preserve lean muscle mass while increasing overall energy expenditure.
WEIGHT_LOSS.recommendedFrequency: 2-3 sessions per muscle group per week
WEIGHT_LOSS.progressionStrategy: Maintain strength while gradually increasing workload.
WEIGHT_LOSS.warmupRecommendation: General warm-up followed by dynamic mobility.
WEIGHT_LOSS.failureGuidelines: Avoid excessive failure to maintain recovery while in a calorie deficit.
notes: Resistance training should remain the priority.
Cardio complements but does not replace strength training.
Protein intake is critical for muscle retention.

GENERAL_FITNESS.primaryObjective: Improve overall health, movement quality and long-term physical fitness.
GENERAL_FITNESS.recommendedFrequency: 2 sessions per muscle group per week
GENERAL_FITNESS.tempo: Controlled with full range of motion
GENERAL_FITNESS.progressionStrategy: Gradually increase repetitions, load or exercise complexity.
GENERAL_FITNESS.warmupRecommendation: 5-10 minutes of light cardio followed by dynamic mobility.
GENERAL_FITNESS.failureGuidelines: Avoid failure on most exercises. Focus on quality movement.
